Import necessary libraries including TensorFlow, imageio, io, and ipywidgets
Define input shape for videos as (batch_size, height, width, channels) where batch_size=32, height=100, width=100, and channels=3
Create random video data with the defined input shape using NumPy
Initialize TubeletEmbedding layer with embed_dim=PROJECTION_DIM=768 and patch_size=PATCH_SIZE=(5, 5)
Apply the initialized TubeletEmbedding layer to the random video data
Apply the initialized PositionalEncoder layer to the output of the TubeletEmbedding layer
Create a transformer block using the specified configuration including num_heads=NUM_HEADS, embed_dim=PROJECTION_DIM, and transformer_layers=NUM_LAYERS
Stack multiple transformer blocks to form the backbone of the ViViT model
Initialize the ViViT model with the tubelet_embedder, positional_encoder, and transformer block
Compile the model using Adam optimizer with learning_rate=LEARNING_RATE
Call the fit method on the compiled model using the training data (trainloader)
Verify that the loss during training contains NaN values by checking if any of the trainable variables have NaN gradients
Check GPU memory usage during model training by querying TensorFlow's runtime information
Assert that GPU memory usage exceeds a certain threshold indicating potential memory overflow or other issues
